# For building iOS app
The release.yml script uses the ```--no-codesign flag```, so it bypasses the need for Apple Developer certificates (see line ~56 of ```flutter_build.yml```). 

However, the build will still fail unless your local Flutter project has its metadata correctly structured for iOS. To fix this:

1. Run pod install locally: Ensure you have opened your terminal and run 

```terminal
cd ios && pod install
```

at least once locally to generate the correct Podfiles. Commit any changes to the repository.

2. Set a Bundle Identifier: Our project must have a valid bundle ID (e.g., ```com.yourname.appname```). You can set this in Xcode or inside the ```ios/Runner.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj``` file.

On top of that, since our repository is private, GitHub gives us a set amount of free Action minutes per month. macOS runners consume those minutes at 10x the rate of Linux runners. As such, since we are just testing, I will comment out the build-ios section to save minutes. Just uncomment when you want to build the iOS version alongside the Andriod version too.

## ⚠️ Note on App Store Deployment
The ```.ipa``` generated by the script is ideal for ad-hoc sharing, manual testing, or archival purposes. When planning to submit the app to the Apple App Store or TestFlight, do not use ```--no-codesign```. GitHub will need access to premium Apple Developer Account certificates.
